summary: Accepted design: parallel work should use first-class scoped change claims/leases across knowledge, roadmap, code, builds, and validation, not task-only ownership. Roadmap tasks remain durable work truth; claims are temporary concurrency leases that graph/status use to warn or block overlaps.

accepted_decisions.summary: Add first-class scoped change claims independent of roadmap tasks, with optional task/build anchors.
Claims may reserve arbitrary scopes across knowledge, roadmap, code, builds, validation, and graph/source layers.
Graph/status should detect overlaps: read/read ok, read/write warn, write/write conflict unless same session or explicit override.
Claims are temporary leases with expiry and release, not requirements, history, or source of truth.
Roadmap task ownership is not enough; tasks remain work truth while claims coordinate concurrent sessions.

assumptions: A small first slice can persist claims in the existing append-only CodeWiki event stream and derive active claim state during rebuild.
Expired claims should be ignored by active-state views without requiring destructive cleanup.
non_goals: Do not implement distributed locking across machines in this first slice.
Do not make claims replace roadmap tasks, builds, validation, git, or code review.

knowledge: Document claim semantics in system API/roadmap/graph/adapters and agent story.
roadmap: Create implementation task for scoped change claims.
code: Add claim domain types, Pi tool schema/implementation, event persistence, status/graph derivation, smoke tests.
